name: spec-driven-development
description: Spec-Driven Development: route intent into specs, plans, tasks, traceability, implementation, and proof.

interface.shortDescription: Turn intent into specs, plans, tasks, implementation, and proof.
interface.longDescription: Practical SDD operating model for any host agent. Routes work across tiny, Spec Kit, Kiro-style, OpenSpec-style, brownfield, bugfix, proposal, and orchestration lanes; separates requirements, design, tasks, code, and proof; and requires traceability plus fresh external evidence before completion claims.
capabilities: SDD lane and evidence-profile selection for tiny fixes, feature work, bugfixes, brownfield changes, and large initiatives
GitHub Spec Kit workflow integration without hiding the underlying project artifacts
Requirement quality ledgers for sources, assumptions, ambiguity, non-goals, acceptance, and success metrics
Technical planning with design, contracts, data models, quickstarts, and LLM-use constraints
Traceable tasks with dependencies, parallel boundaries, requirement links, changed paths, and expected evidence
Implementation from tasks with spec drift handling, evidence ledgers, and external verification
Offline validators for SDD surface discovery, traceability, completed-task evidence, and risky LLM self-judge claims
defaultPrompt: Use Spec-Driven Development to choose the right SDD lane for this repository and feature.
Use Spec-Driven Development with GitHub Spec Kit for this feature.
Use Spec-Driven Development to audit the spec, plan, tasks, and implementation proof before coding or claiming done.
